revision.text: #redirect[[en:Takaya_Tsukiyama_Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''高屋城山古墳'''（たかやしろやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目にあり、[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する[[前方後円墳]]。'''安閑天皇陵'''とも。陵名は'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるいちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）で、第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[陵墓]]に治定されている。
==概要==
高屋城山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100メートル・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの前方後円墳である。
古墳の名前は、中世（室町時代後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の本丸がおかれていたことに由来する。
出土した円筒埴輪の特徴から、6世紀初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。
宮内庁によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が国道170号線(旧道)に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.mapion.co.jp/c/f?uc=1&grp=all&nl=34/32/43.819&el=135/36/43.939&scl=25000&bid=Mlink 地図（mapionより）] *[http://maps.google.com/maps?ie=UTF8&om=1&z=18&ll=34.5489,135.609446&spn=0.002041,0.003074&t=k google.mapsの衛星画像]
==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
[[category:古墳|たかやしろやまこふん]] [[category:日本の陵墓|たかやしろやまこふん]] [[category:羽曳野市|たかやしろやまこふん]]

revision.comment: 改名伴い若干の文章変更
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=高屋築山古墳| 画像=[[Image:Grave of Emperor Ankan.jpg|250px]]| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市古市5丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56|N|135|36|34.62|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳丘長122m、後円部直径78ｍ・高さ13ｍ、前方部幅100m・高さ12.5ｍ| 築造年代=6世紀初頭| 被葬者=[[安閑天皇]]（[[宮内庁]]治定）| 出土品=円筒埴輪| 史跡指定=宮内庁治定（古市高屋丘陵）| }}
'''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目にあり、[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する[[前方後円墳]]。'''安閑天皇陵'''とも。陵名は'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるいちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）で、第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[陵墓]]に治定されている。
== 概要 ==
[[画像:Takayaj3.jpg|thumb|left|高屋築山古墳の前方]]
高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100メートル・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。
この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の本丸がおれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。
出土した[[埴輪|円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。
[[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[天皇陵]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳/羽曳野市公式ホームページ] * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 安閑天皇古市高屋丘陵/天皇陵公式ホームページ] * [http://map.yahoo.co.jp/pl?type=scroll&lat=34.54561861&lon=135.6124833&sc=3&mode=map&pointer=on 高屋築山古墳周辺地図/YAHOO!地図情報]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=高屋築山古墳| 画像=[[Image:Grave of Emperor Ankan.jpg|250px]]| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市古市5丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56|N|135|36|34.62|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳丘長122m、後円部直径78ｍ・高さ13ｍ、前方部幅100m・高さ12.5ｍ| 築造年代=6世紀初頭| 被葬者=[[安閑天皇]]（[[宮内庁]]治定）| 出土品=円筒埴輪| 史跡指定=宮内庁治定（古市高屋丘陵）| }}
'''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目にあり、[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する[[前方後円墳]]。'''安閑天皇陵'''とも。陵名は'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるいちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）で、第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[陵墓]]に治定されている。
== 概要 ==
[[画像:Takayaj3.jpg|thumb|left|高屋築山古墳の前方]] [[画像:Takayak.jpg|thumb|left|高屋築山古墳の空中写真]]
高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100メートル・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。
この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の本丸がおれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。
出土した[[埴輪|円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。
[[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。{{-}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[天皇陵]]

高さ13m| 築造年代=6世紀初頭| 被葬者=第27代[[安閑天皇]]（[[宮内庁]]治定）| 出土品=円筒埴輪| 史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定（古市高屋丘陵）| }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[画像:Takayaj3.jpg|thumb|left|高屋築山古墳の前方]] [[画像:Takayak.jpg|thumb|left|高屋築山古墳の空中写真]] 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100メートル・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の本丸がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。 出土した[[埴輪|円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。{{-}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[天皇陵]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳/羽曳野市公式ホームページ] * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 安閑天皇古市高屋丘陵/宮内庁公式ホームページ] * [http://map.yahoo.co.jp/pl?type=scroll&lat=34.54561861&lon=135.6124833&sc=3&mode=map&pointer=on 高屋築山古墳周辺地図/YAHOO!地図情報] *[http://goryou.fc2web.com/goryou03/027.html 安閑天皇陵参拝案内] {{デフォルトソート:たかやしろやまこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=高屋築山古墳| 画像=[[Image:Grave of Emperor Ankan.jpg|250px]]| 所属=[[古市古墳群]]| 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}}| 形状=[[前方後円墳]]| 規模=墳丘長122m
高さ13m| 築造年代=[[6世紀]]初頭| 被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第27代[[安閑天皇]]| 出土品=[[円筒埴輪]]| 史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「古市高屋丘陵」| }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100メートル・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の本丸がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。 出土した[[埴輪|円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 画像:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 画像:Takayaj3.jpg|前方部 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳]（羽曳野市ホームページ） * [http://map.yahoo.co.jp/pl?type=scroll&lat=34.54561861&lon=135.6124833&sc=3&mode=map&pointer=on 高屋築山古墳周辺地図/YAHOO!地図情報] {{デフォルトソート:たかやしろやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=高屋築山古墳| 画像=[[Image:Grave of Emperor Ankan.jpg|250px]]| 所属=[[古市古墳群]]| 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}}| 形状=[[前方後円墳]]| 規模=墳丘長122m
高さ13m| 築造年代=[[6世紀]]初頭| 被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第27代[[安閑天皇]]| 出土品=[[円筒埴輪]]| 指定文化財=[[宮内庁]]治定「古市高屋丘陵」| }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100メートル・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の本丸がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。 出土した[[埴輪|円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 画像:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 画像:Takayaj3.jpg|前方部 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳]（羽曳野市ホームページ） * [http://map.yahoo.co.jp/pl?type=scroll&lat=34.54561861&lon=135.6124833&sc=3&mode=map&pointer=on 高屋築山古墳周辺地図/YAHOO!地図情報] {{デフォルトソート:たかやしろやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=高屋築山古墳| 画像=[[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部）| 所属=[[古市古墳群]]| 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}}| 形状=[[前方後円墳]]| 規模=墳丘長122m
高さ13m| 築造年代=[[6世紀]]初頭| 被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第27代[[安閑天皇]]・神前皇女| 出土品=[[円筒埴輪]]| 指定文化財=[[宮内庁]]治定「古市高屋丘陵」| }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されているほか、合葬の神前皇女（[[継体天皇]]皇女）の墓に治定されている。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100メートル・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の本丸がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。 出土した[[埴輪|円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 画像:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[安閑天皇]]古市高屋丘陵・神前皇女墓 拝所 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやしろやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=高屋築山古墳 |画像=[[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長122[[メートル|m]]
高さ13m |築造年代=[[6世紀]]初頭 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第27代[[安閑天皇]]・神前皇女 |出土品=[[円筒埴輪]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「古市高屋丘陵」 }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されているほか、合葬の神前皇女（[[継体天皇]]皇女）の墓に治定されている。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100[[メートル]]・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の[[本丸]]がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。なお､『足利季世紀』によれば､[[畠山稙長]]は｢｢本城ニハ恐レ｣て居住せず｢二ノ丸｣に居住している｡古墳に対する畏敬の念がありながらも､城として利用していることは興味深いと指摘されている[[竹井英文]]『戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―』(吉川弘文館､2018念)48-49頁｡ 出土した[[円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 画像:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[安閑天皇]]古市高屋丘陵・神前皇女墓 拝所 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやしろやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=高屋築山古墳 |画像=[[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長122[[メートル|m]]
高さ13m |築造年代=[[6世紀]]初頭 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第27代[[安閑天皇]]・神前皇女 |出土品=[[円筒埴輪]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「古市高屋丘陵」 }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]古市5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されているほか、合葬の神前皇女（[[継体天皇]]皇女）の墓に治定されている。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100[[メートル]]・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の[[本丸]]がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。なお、『足利季世紀』によれば、[[畠山稙長]]は「本城ニハ恐レ」て居住せず「二ノ丸」に居住している。古墳に対する畏敬の念がありながらも城として利用していることは興味深いと指摘されている{{Cite book|和書|author=竹井英文|authorlink=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}。 出土した[[円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 画像:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[安閑天皇]]古市高屋丘陵・神前皇女墓 拝所 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやつきやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやつきやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=高屋築山古墳 |画像=[[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長122[[メートル|m]]
高さ13m |築造年代=[[6世紀]]初頭 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第27代[[安閑天皇]]・神前皇女・春日山田皇女 |出土品=[[円筒埴輪]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「古市高屋丘陵」 }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されているほか、合葬の[[神前皇女]]（[[継体天皇]]皇女）・[[春日山田皇女]]（[[仁賢天皇]]皇女）の墓に治定されている。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100[[メートル]]・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の[[本丸]]がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。なお、『足利季世紀』によれば、[[畠山稙長]]は「本城ニハ恐レ」て居住せず「二ノ丸」に居住している。古墳に対する畏敬の念がありながらも城として利用していることは興味深いと指摘されている{{Cite book|和書|author=竹井英文|authorlink=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}。 出土した[[円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 File:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[安閑天皇]]古市高屋丘陵・[[神前皇女]]墓 拝所 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやつきやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

revision.comment: [[H:RV|取り消し]]：現在は春日山田皇女古市高屋陵（[[高屋八幡山古墳]]）とは別。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=高屋築山古墳 |画像=[[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長122[[メートル|m]]
高さ13m |築造年代=[[6世紀]]初頭 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第27代[[安閑天皇]]・神前皇女 |出土品=[[円筒埴輪]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「古市高屋丘陵」 }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されているほか、合葬の[[神前皇女]]（[[継体天皇]]皇女）の墓に治定されている。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100[[メートル]]・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の[[本丸]]がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。なお、『足利季世紀』によれば、[[畠山稙長]]は「本城ニハ恐レ」て居住せず「二ノ丸」に居住している。古墳に対する畏敬の念がありながらも城として利用していることは興味深いと指摘されている{{Cite book|和書|author=竹井英文|authorlink=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}。 出土した[[円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 File:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[安閑天皇]]古市高屋丘陵・[[神前皇女]]墓 拝所 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやつきやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

revision.comment: 国の史跡に係る事柄について。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=高屋築山古墳 |画像=[[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長122[[メートル|m]]

[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやつきやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長122[[メートル|m]]
高さ13m |築造年代=[[6世紀]]初頭 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第27代[[安閑天皇]]・神前皇女 |出土品=[[円筒埴輪]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「古市高屋丘陵」 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「安閑天皇陵周堤」
（「古市古墳群」に包含） }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されているほか、合葬の[[神前皇女]]（[[継体天皇]]皇女）の墓に治定されている。また周堤は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100[[メートル]]・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の[[本丸]]がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。なお、『足利季世紀』によれば、[[畠山稙長]]は「本城ニハ恐レ」て居住せず「二ノ丸」に居住している。古墳に対する畏敬の念がありながらも城として利用していることは興味深いと指摘されている{{Cite book|和書|author=竹井英文|authorlink=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}。 出土した[[円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 File:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[安閑天皇]]古市高屋丘陵・[[神前皇女]]墓 拝所 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 安閑天皇陵周堤（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち） - 2024年（令和6年）10月11日指定[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html 文化審議会の答申（史跡名勝天然記念物の指定等）]（文化庁報道発表 、2024年6月24日）。令和6年10月11日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやつきやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: Created page with "[[ja:高屋築山古墳]]"
revision.text: [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Ancient Japanese Burial Mound | Name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | Image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | Name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

revision.text:
[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient burial mounds in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | Belongs to = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | Excavated items = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been designated a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} *
