revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National other_designation: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National other_designation === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of other_designations, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Habikino]] While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National other_designation === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of other_designations, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National other_designation: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | Cultural Property = National other_designation: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important Cultural Property), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == Cultural Property == === National other_designation === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of other_designations, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National other_designation: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == designation1 == === National other_designation === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (Designation of other_designations, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} other_designation) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National other_designation: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been other_designation1 a National other_designation as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == designation1 == === National other_designation === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of other_designations, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}[[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takayatukiyama Kofun}}

According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{citation needed|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{citation needed|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] {{Kofun navbar}}

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}. Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] {{citation needed|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=draft|12=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]
