Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=draft|12=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

revision.comment: 44 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:高屋築山古墳 up to rev 102175559
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=高屋築山古墳 |画像=[[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=高屋築山古墳（伝安閑天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長122[[メートル|m]]
高さ13m |築造年代=[[6世紀]]初頭 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第27代[[安閑天皇]]・神前皇女 |出土品=[[円筒埴輪]] |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「古市高屋丘陵」 |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「安閑天皇陵周堤」
（「古市古墳群」に包含） }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されているほか、合葬の[[神前皇女]]（[[継体天皇]]皇女）の墓に治定されている。また周堤は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100[[メートル]]・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の[[本丸]]がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。なお、『足利季世紀』によれば、[[畠山稙長]]は「本城ニハ恐レ」て居住せず「二ノ丸」に居住している。古墳に対する畏敬の念がありながらも城として利用していることは興味深いと指摘されている{{Cite book|和書|author=竹井英文|authorlink=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}。 出土した[[円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 File:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[安閑天皇]]古市高屋丘陵・[[神前皇女]]墓 拝所 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 安閑天皇陵周堤（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち） - 2024年（令和6年）10月11日指定[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html 文化審議会の答申（史跡名勝天然記念物の指定等）]（文化庁報道発表 、2024年6月24日）。令和6年10月11日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやつきやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

（「古市古墳群」に包含） }} '''高屋築山古墳'''（たかやつきやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]][[古市 (大阪市)|古市]]5丁目にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]の最南端に位置する。「'''安閑天皇陵'''」とも。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''古市高屋丘陵'''（ふるちのたかやのおかのみささぎ）」として第27代[[安閑天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されているほか、合葬の[[神前皇女]]（[[継体天皇]]皇女）の墓に治定されている。また周堤は国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 高屋築山古墳は、墳丘の長さ122メートル、前方部の幅100[[メートル]]・高さ12.5メートル、後円部は直径78メートル・高さ13メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 この古墳は、中世（[[室町時代]]後半）に[[畠山氏]]の居城である[[高屋城]]の[[本丸]]がおかれており元々の形態が大きく改変されている。なお、『足利季世紀』によれば、[[畠山稙長]]は「本城ニハ恐レ」て居住せず「二ノ丸」に居住している。古墳に対する畏敬の念がありながらも城として利用していることは興味深いと指摘されている{{Cite book|和書|author=竹井英文|authorlink=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}。 出土した[[円筒埴輪]]の特徴から、[[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたものと考えられている。 [[宮内庁]]によって安閑天皇陵に治定されており、拝所が[[国道170号]]線（旧道）に面した前方部正面の濠の外にある。 File:Takayak.jpg|古墳の空中写真 File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|[[安閑天皇]]古市高屋丘陵・[[神前皇女]]墓 拝所 ファイル:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|伝安閑陵出土 ガラス碗（国の[[重要文化財]]）
[[東京国立博物館]]展示。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 安閑天皇陵周堤（史跡「古市古墳群」のうち） - 2024年（令和6年）10月11日指定[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html 文化審議会の答申（史跡名勝天然記念物の指定等）]（文化庁報道発表 、2024年6月24日）。令和6年10月11日文部科学省告示第144号。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html 古市高屋丘陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html 高屋築山古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:たかやつきやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高屋築山古墳|version=102175559|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高屋築山古墳|version=102175559|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ankan|qq=}} and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ankan|qq=}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|qq=}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高屋築山古墳|version=102175559|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]]

It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|qq=}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高屋築山古墳|version=102175559|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|qq=}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高屋築山古墳|version=102175559|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]]

File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → National historic sites in Osaka prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|qq=}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高屋築山古墳|version=102175559|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|qq=}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高屋築山古墳|version=102175559|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Takayatukiyama Kofun}}

File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高屋築山古墳|version=102175559|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Habikino]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Takayatukiyama Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|qq=}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] == designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takayatukiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/027/index.html Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.city.habikino.osaka.jp/info/115/bunkazai/iseki/kouki/t_k_ankan.html Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun] - Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|高屋築山古墳|version=102175559|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun]] [[ja:高屋築山古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Takayatukiyama Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = [[File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|250px]]
Kofun (front mound on the right, rear mound in the back left) | part_of = [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, Furuichi 5-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|56.00|N|135|36|34.62|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) }} '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan." While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|qq=}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}} Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170. '''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.

== Overview ==

The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.

During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|qq=|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>

Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.

<gallery>
File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus
File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave
File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>

'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.

== Overview ==

The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.

During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|qq=|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>

Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.

<gallery>
File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus
File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave
File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>

== designation1 ==
=== National Historic Site ===
* Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.<ref>[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).</ref><ref>October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144.</ref>

'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.

== Overview ==

The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.

During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>

Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.

<gallery>
File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus
File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave
File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>

== designation1 ==
=== National Historic Site ===
* Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.<ref>[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).</ref><ref>October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144.</ref>

'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.

== Overview ==

The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.

During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>

Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.

<gallery>
File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus
File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave
File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>

== designation1 ==
=== National Historic Site ===
* Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.<ref>[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).</ref><ref>October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144.</ref>

'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.

== Overview ==

The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.

During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>

Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.

<gallery>
File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus
File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave
File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>

== designation1 ==
=== National Historic Site ===
* Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.<ref>[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).</ref><ref>October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144.</ref>

'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.

== Overview ==

The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.

During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>

Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.

<gallery>
File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus
File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave
File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>

== designation1 ==
=== National Historic Site ===
* Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.<ref>[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).</ref><ref>October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144.</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun | image = File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}} | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.548889|N|135.609617|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] Historic Site) [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki | excavations = Cylindrical haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵) | designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Ankan|WD=Q241765}}; {{ill|Kansaki no Himemiko|WD=Q96715460}} }}
'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.
== Overview ==
The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.
During the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>
Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.
<gallery> File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>
== designation1 == === National Historic Site === * Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.<ref>[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).</ref><ref>October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144.</ref>
