'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.

== Overview ==

The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.

During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>

| area = Total length: 122 meters; height: 13 meters

| Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} Historic Site) {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}} and Princess Kanzaki
| excavations = Cylindrical haniwa
| Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum" (古市高屋丘陵)
| designation1 = National Historic Site: "Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb" (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster)
| architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}
| commemorates = {{ill|Ankan|WD=Q241765}}; {{ill|Kansaki no Himemiko|WD=Q96715460}}
}}

'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.

== Overview ==

The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.

During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>

Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.

<gallery>
File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus
File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave
File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}
</gallery>

== designation1 ==
=== National Historic Site ===
* Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.<ref>[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).</ref><ref>October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144.</ref>

'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.
== Overview ==
The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.
During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>
Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.

'''Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun''' (高屋築山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the southernmost section of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. It is also known as the "Mausoleum of Emperor Ankan."

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the "Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum," attributed to {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}} and Princess Kanzaki, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}. The surrounding moat has been Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster.

== Overview ==

The Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun has a total length of 122 meters, with the front mound measuring 100 meters in width and 12.5 meters in height, while the rear mound has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters.

During the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the tumulus served as the site of the main keep (honmaru) of {{ill|Takaya Castle|ja|高屋城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Castle|1=Takaya Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669272}}, controlled by the Hatakeyama clan. This adaptation significantly altered the original structure. According to historical records, Hatakeyama Takamasa avoided residing in the "main castle" out of fear and instead lived in the "second keep," reflecting a blend of reverence for and practical use of the tumulus.<ref>{{Cite book|author=竹井英文|title=戦国の城の一生―つくる・壊す・蘇る―|publisher=吉川弘文館|year=2018|pages=48-49}}</ref>

Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} unearthed at the site suggest that the tumulus was constructed in the early 6th century. The Imperial Household Agency recognizes it as Emperor Ankan's tomb, with a worship area located on the front mound, facing the old route of National Route 170.

<gallery>
File:Takayak.jpg|Aerial view of the tumulus
File:Takaya Tsukiyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|Worship area of Emperor Ankan's Furuichi Takaya Hill Mausoleum and Princess Kanzaki's grave
File:伝安閑陵古墳出土 ガラス碗.JPG|Glass bowl excavated from the site (National Important designation1), displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}
</gallery>

== designation1 ==
=== National Historic Site ===
* Moat of Emperor Ankan's Tomb (part of the Furuichi Kofun Cluster) - Designated on October 11, 2024.<ref>[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/94070501.html Cultural Council's Recommendation (designation1 of Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments)] (Agency for Cultural Affairs press release, June 24, 2024).</ref><ref>October 11, 2024, Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144.</ref>
